observations.title: Session summary: examya
observations.content:
## Goal
Blindaje y estabilización del flujo de órdenes médicas del agente Shuri en WhatsApp.

## Instructions
- Se debe usar el branch-pr skill para PRs (hecho).
- TDD es mandatorio para tareas de Shuri.
- El precio de la orden médica a través de Shuri es siempre $5.000 CLP (FLAT_ORDER_FEE).

## Discoveries
- El flujo de Shuri tenía una regresión circular entre confirmación y cupón que causaba loops infinitos.
- `node_modules` local tiene un problema de `ELOOP` que bloquea Vitest en el workspace (`realpath` failure).
- La pérdida de `suggestedExamCodes` ocurría porque el handler recreaba el objeto de datos sin mapear el contexto previo.
- La confirmación de pago era frágil: cualquier respuesta no afirmativa disparaba un reset a START. Se mejoró con una etapa de aclaración.

## Accomplished
- ✅ Corregido loop infinito en `purchase.handler.ts` introduciendo la etapa `awaiting_payment`.
- ✅ Implementado precio fijo de $5.000 CLP forzado en el handler.
- ✅ Creada suite de tests unitarios completa en `purchase.handler.spec.ts`.
- ✅ Aplicado protocolo de "Judgment Day" con Round 2 de mejoras en resiliencia de pagos y triage de errores de API.
- ✅ Creado PR #296 a `devops`.

## Next Steps
- Limpiar el entorno local (`rm -rf node_modules && pnpm install`) para recuperar la capacidad de ejecución de tests.
- Conectar la llamada real al bridge en `handlePayment` para la creación persistente de la orden.

## Relevant Files
- apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/handlers/purchase.handler.ts — Lógica de estados y precios.
- apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/handlers/purchase.handler.spec.ts — Suite de blindaje.

observations.title: Fixed Shuri confirmation stage regression and flat fee
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed 3 chained bugs in PurchaseHandler that caused WhatsApp order flow to fail after symptom-based exam suggestions.
**Why**: Bot was responding "No pude identificar los exámenes" after user confirmed a quote because: (1) `awaiting_confirmation` stage fell through to `handleQuote()` instead of transitioning to coupon flow, (2) FONASA prices were summed instead of flat $5.000 CLP, (3) `quoteByCodes()` overwrote `suggestedExamCodes` in data payload.
**Where**: `apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/handlers/purchase.handler.ts` — added `handleConfirmation()`, `FLAT_ORDER_FEE = 5000`, preserved `suggestedExamCodes` in `quoteByCodes()` data.
**Learned**: The FSM stage routing in `handle()` was incomplete — only checked for `awaiting_coupon` but not `awaiting_confirmation`. Always verify that EVERY stage listed in the state machine's routing table has a corresponding handler branch. Data payloads between FSM transitions can be silently overwritten if not carefully preserved.

observations.title: Fixed WhatsApp bot 0 exams $0 on order creation
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed WhatsApp bot returning "0 exámenes, $0" when user confirms order creation after symptom-based exam suggestion.
**Why**: Two bugs in the purchase flow: (1) ExamCatalogService.searchExams() received comma-separated FONASA codes as a single string and tried `contains` matching, finding nothing. (2) PurchaseHandler treated the `total` field (record count) as a monetary amount.
**Where**: apps/api/src/modules/medical-order-sales/services/exam-catalog.service.ts — Added multi-code detection that splits comma-separated codes and uses Prisma `in` operator. apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/handlers/purchase.handler.ts — Fixed both quoteByCodes() and handleQuote() to sum level1TotalValue from items instead of using the count field.
**Learned**: The searchExams endpoint was designed for text search only. When agents send multiple FONASA codes like "0301045,0308004", it needs to detect that pattern and switch to an exact IN query. The `total` field in ExamSearchResult is a count, not a price — confusing naming that will keep causing bugs.

observations.title: Fixed greeting not resetting stale Shuri sessions
observations.content:
**What**: Removed `!purchase.stage` guard from greeting check in shuri-agent.ts so greetings always reset the session, plus added POST /health/session/clear endpoint.

**Why**: After a symptom flow, `purchase.stage = 'awaiting_exam_list'` persisted in Redis. When user sent "hola", the guard `if (isGreeting && !purchase.stage)` FAILED because stage was truthy, causing "hola" to fall through to FSM → PurchaseHandler → "0 exámenes, $0".

**Where**: apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/shuri-agent.ts (line 245, GREET-02), apps/agents/src/routes/health.ts (new session/clear endpoint)

**Learned**: A greeting is always a session-reset signal. Never gate greetings on session state — the user saying "hola" means "start fresh". The only stage that survives past the greeting guard is human_handoff, which is already resolved at the top of processMessage.

observations.title: Session summary: examya
observations.content:
## Goal
Fix Shuri WhatsApp bot end-to-end flow: 5 bugs identified, 4 fixed, 1 remaining (stale session state).

## Instructions
- Código y comentarios en Inglés; comunicación en Español
- Conventional Commits format
- KISS & DRY principles
- Medical Privacy: no PII logging

## Discoveries
- ShuriStateMachine.createBridge() returned {} instead of singleton → TypeError
- Redis handoff key TTL was 24h, blocking all messages across deploys
- PurchaseHandler ignored suggestedExamCodes from SymptomHandler
- extractExamList had no Spanish stopwords, validateExamCodes accepted any string >0 chars
- Greeting guard at shuri-agent.ts:245 only checks `!purchase.stage` but stale sessions have stage='awaiting_exam_list' → greeting falls through to FSM → PurchaseHandler.handleQuote("hola") → 0 exams
- Redis session key pattern: `whatsapp:ctx:{phone}` (shuri-memory.ts:176-178), TTL 86400s
- Handoff key pattern: `handoff:{phone}`, was cleared via POST /health/handoff/clear

## Accomplished
- ✅ Bug 1: Fixed ShuriBridge empty object (PR #289 → merged devops)
- ✅ Bug 2: Reduced handoff TTL from 24h→2h + added clear endpoint (committed devops)
- ✅ Bug 3: Fixed unclosed handoff block TS error (PR #290 → merged devops)
- ✅ Bug 4: Fixed symptom→order flow losing exam codes (PR #291 + #292 → merged devops)
- ✅ All 3 services deployed: examya-api, examya-agents, examya-web

## Next Steps
- 🔲 Bug 5: Fix greeting guard in shuri-agent.ts:245 to reset stale purchase stages on greeting
- 🔲 Add POST /health/session/clear endpoint to clear stale Redis sessions
- 🔲 Clear stale Redis session key whatsapp:ctx:56962170366
- 🔲 Re-test WhatsApp end-to-end flow
- 🔲 Clean up stale local branches

## Relevant Files
- apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/shuri-agent.ts — MAIN FILE, needs greeting guard fix at line 245
- apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/shuri-memory.ts — Redis key patterns, session persistence
- apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/handlers/purchase.handler.ts — Fixed with isConfirmation + quoteByCodes
- apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/domain/shuri-extractors.ts — Fixed with stopwords + min length 3
- apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/core/shuri-state-machine.ts — Fixed bridge singleton
- apps/agents/src/routes/health.ts — Handoff clear endpoint, needs session clear too
- apps/web/next.config.mjs — CSP header fix (done)

observations.title: Fixed Shuri symptom→exam_list persisting session
observations.content:
**What**: Added `isConfirmation()` heuristic + `suggestedExamCodes` priority check in PurchaseHandler + stopwords fix in extractExamList + added session session persisted state.
**Why**: Previous session left `purchase.stage = 'awaiting_exam_list'` from symptoms flow. When user said "hola", it the code on devops was all fixes.

**Where**:
- apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/handlers/purchase.handler.ts
- apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/domain/shuri-extractors.ts
- apps/web/next.config.mjs

- **Learned**:
- `isGreeting && !purchase.stage` guard at line 245 of shuri-agent.ts DOES NOT account for stale sessions state. Even though the new greeting is sent, + our `isConfirmation` fix will route to purchase handler first. This is safe because `awaiting_exam_list` can now be confirmed → suggestion flow.
- Purchase stage persists in Redis session memory with TTL 86400s (24h). Another PR merged will NOT clear it. Must clear manually via endpoint or new session clear endpoint added, to health router.

observations.title: Fixed symptom-to-order flow losing suggested exams
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed 3 bugs in symptom-to-order flow where exams suggested by SymptomHandler were lost when user confirmed. Also added next.config.mjs security header change.

**Why**: User tested WhatsApp flow "me duele el estomago" -> "si por favor" and got "0 exams, $0 total" instead of the actual exam prices.

**Where**:
- apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/handlers/purchase.handler.ts — added isConfirmation(), quoteByCodes(), suggestedExamCodes priority in handleQuote()
- apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/domain/shuri-extractors.ts — added Spanish stopwords to extractExamList, raised min length in validateExamCodes
- apps/web/next.config.mjs — replaced X-Frame-Options DENY with Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors

**Learned**:
- SymptomHandler saves suggestedExamCodes in purchase.data but PurchaseHandler.handleQuote() IGNORED this data and tried to extract from the confirmation text
- extractExamList("si por favor") returns ["si por favor"] because "si", "por", "favor" were not in the stopword list
- validateExamCodes accepts ANY string with length > 0 — even "POR FAVOR"
- The pattern is save code in context -> use on confirmation is critical for any multi-turn flow
- PR #292 created to devops with all 3 fixes + next.config.mjs

observations.title: Fixed Shuri symptom→order flow losing exam codes
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed 3 chained bugs where Shuri lost suggested exam codes between symptom report and order confirmation, returning "He encontrado 0 exámenes. El total estimado es $0."

**Why**: When user confirmed "si por favor" after symptom suggestions, PurchaseHandler ignored the `suggestedExamCodes` stored in context and tried to extract exam names from the confirmation text.

**Where**:
- apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/handlers/purchase.handler.ts — Added isConfirmation() heuristic + quoteByCodes() to use pre-suggested codes
- apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/domain/shuri-extractors.ts — Added missing Spanish stopwords to extractExamList, raised validateExamCodes min length to 3

**Learned**:
- State handoff between FSM handlers is fragile — data stored in `purchase.data` by one handler can be silently ignored by the next handler if it doesn't explicitly check for it
- `extractExamList` had incomplete Spanish stopwords allowing "por favor" to pass as an exam name
- `validateExamCodes` accepted any non-empty string including natural language phrases

observations.title: Fixed ShuriBridge empty object causing Shuri fallback
observations.content:
**What**: ShuriStateMachine.createBridge() returned {} instead of the actual shuriBridge singleton, causing TypeError: this.bridge.call is not a function
**Why**: During PR288 FSM refactor, createBridge() was left as a stub returning an empty object
**Where**: apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/core/shuri-state-machine.ts — static createBridge() method
**Learned**: The PurchaseHandler (and other handlers) receive bridge via constructor injection from ShuriAgent, which calls ShuriStateMachine.createBridge(). If bridge is {}, every this.bridge.call() throws TypeError, triggering fallback to generic ExamIA greeting. The error was visible in production logs at purchase.handler.js:38:49.

observations.title: Session summary: marioSoftmedic/examya
observations.content:
## Goal
Hardening final del Agente Shuri para producción, enfocándose en seguridad (PII), consistencia de tono (Chile/Neutro) e integridad de datos.

## Instructions
- Tono estándar: Profesional, neutro o chileno (STRICT: No usar voseo rioplatense).
- Seguridad: Redacción obligatoria de PII en todas las salidas externas (Slack, Email, Logs).
- Integridad: Validaciones de nombres estrictas para evitar datos basura en el CRM.

## Discoveries
- `isGarbageName` fue extraído a `shuri-utils.ts` para evitar duplicación y fallos de validación inconsistentes entre handlers y campañas.
- La redacción de PII en notificaciones de Slack requería intervención directa en `shuri-agent.ts` sobre el objeto de notificación antes del envío.
- El voseo estaba presente no solo en mensajes, sino también en prompts del sistema y mensajes de error.

## Accomplished
- ✅ **Seguridad**: Blindaje de PII en Slack (`shuri-agent.ts`), Email y logs (`CampaignNotificationService`).
- ✅ **Tono**: Purga total de voseo en `Purchase`, `Profile`, `Scheduling` y `Campaign` handlers.
- ✅ **Integridad**: Refuerzo de `isGarbageName` (rechazo de números/símbolos) y uso en flujos de campaña.
- ✅ **Lógica**: Integración de validación de cupones en `PurchaseHandler`.
- ✅ **Verificación**: 96 tests pasados (excluyendo timeouts externos) y `type-check` limpio.

## Next Steps
- El agente está listo para producción (Veredicto: **CLEAN**).
- Monitorear logs redactados para asegurar que no haya falsos positivos excesivos en la redacción de nombres.

## Relevant Files
- `apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/domain/shuri-utils.ts` — Centralización de PII y Garbage filters.
- `apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/shuri-agent.ts` — Orquestador y seguridad Slack.
- `apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/handlers/*.handler.ts` — Lógica de negocio con tono corregido.
- `apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/campaign-intent-detector.ts` — Flujos de campaña con validación de nombre.

observations.title: Fixed React is not defined in SSR prerender
observations.content:
**What**: Added `import React` to NewsFeed.tsx to fix "React is not defined" error during Astro static prerender
**Why**: Astro 6 with React 18 automatic JSX runtime works in browser but fails during SSR prerender — React must be explicitly in scope
**Where**: src/components/NewsFeed.tsx (line 1)
**Learned**: Always import React explicitly in Astro React islands when using `output: 'static'` — the automatic JSX runtime doesn't apply during prerender

observations.title: Session summary: examya
observations.content:
## Goal
Fix 3 interconnected WhatsApp bot bugs in Shuri agent's suggestedExamCodes flow, then create a clean PR to devops.

 Also add RUT guard + error handling fixes to resume flow and optional chaining fix `dead code removal`. Stray comma fix + 2 rounds of Judgment Day adversarial review completed ( 40/41 tests passing, TypeScript compiles clean. Diff: +78/-7.

 PR #287 created and merged to devops.

 All confirmed issues from Round 2 review applied and all remaining issues deferred to follow-up PR.

## Discoveries
- Oracle agent consistently times out (3/3) — use librarian agents only for Judgment day
- `purchase.data` is typed as `any` — boundary validation at consumption point is critical

observations.title: Configured all delegates to use glm-5.1 instead of Anthropic Claude
observations.content:
**What**: Added `delegate`, `delegation_list`, `delegation_read` tools to gentleman agent, and updated Judgment Day SKILL.md to force `agent: "gentleman"` on ALL delegate() calls. Also changed language from Rioplatense to Chilean southern Spanish.

**Why**: Delegates were using OpenCode's built-in `general` subagent which inherits the session's default model. If OpenCode fell back to Anthropic Claude, it failed with "no balance" error because the user has Anthropic Max (subscription for claude.ai) but no API credit at console.anthropic.com. The API key `sk-ant-api03-...` is separate billing from the Max subscription.

**Where**: 
- `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json` — added delegate tools to gentleman agent
- `~/.config/opencode/skills/judgment-day/SKILL.md` — added agent: "gentleman" requirement in Pattern 1, Rules, Blocking Rules, and Commands sections

**Learned**: 
- Anthropic Max subscription ≠ API key billing. Two completely separate billing systems.
- OpenCode's delegate() spawns built-in subagents that don't respect custom agent model configs. Must explicitly pass agent: "gentleman" to delegate calls.
- Antigravity plugin has limited Claude quota too — can also cause "no balance" errors.

observations.title: Engram project name is case-sensitive — always use lowercase
observations.content:
**What**: Engram's project filter is CASE-SENSITIVE. The Examya project is stored as `examya` (lowercase). Searching with `project: "Examya"` returns 0 results.

**Why**: Discovered when mem_search returned "No memories found" but data existed — was querying with capital E.

**Where**: All mem_search calls with project filter

**Learned**: ALWAYS use lowercase `examya` when filtering by project. When mem_search returns 0 results, retry without project filter or with lowercase before concluding "no data exists". Also try mem_context first (which doesn't filter by project name).

observations.title: Fixed 5 interconnected platform bugs after WhatsApp investigation
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed 5 interconnected bugs reported after WhatsApp conversation test.

**Where**: 
- `apps/web/src/lib/analytics/backend-proxy.ts` — getServerSession conditional for requireAuth
- `apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/shuri-agent.ts` — RUT DB fallback by phone lookup

9. Supabase project `zsskhqgslpobwbnymxii` has 13 migrations recorded but local and remote that synced

observations.title: Diagnosed 5 interconnected platform bugs
observations.content:
**What**: Diagnosed 5 interconnected bugs reported after WhatsApp test: SQL isActive error, quota page failure, analytics auth error, WhatsApp order generation failure, unnecessary RUT prompt.

**Why**: User tested WhatsApp conversation flow and found multiple failures across the platform.

**Where**: 
- `apps/api/src/modules/quota/infrastructure/repositories/prisma-user-quota.repository.ts` — raw SQL uses `is_active` (correct), but DB may have schema drift
- `apps/web/src/lib/analytics/backend-proxy.ts` line 42 — `getServerSession()` runs even when `requireAuth: false`
- `apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/handlers/quote_medical_order.cjs` — order creation via ShuriBridge
- `apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/service-token.cjs` — needs SHURI_SERVICE_CLIENT_ID/SECRET env vars
- `apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/shuri-agent.ts` line 2221 — RUT resolution from context

**Learned**:
1. BUG 1 (SQL isActive): All Prisma schemas have correct `@map("is_active")`, but likely unapplied migrations cause schema drift. Need `prisma migrate status`.
2. BUG 2 (Quota page): Cascades from BUG 1. `getQuotaMetrics()` catch returns generic error.
3. BUG 3 (Analytics auth): `getServerSession(authOptions)` in `backend-proxy.ts` runs unconditionally at line 42, even when `requireAuth: false`. If authOptions is misconfigured, it throws before checking requireAuth. Fix: move inside `if (requireAuth)` block.
4. BUG 4 (WhatsApp order): Either `SHURI_SERVICE_CLIENT_ID`/`SHURI_SERVICE_CLIENT_SECRET` missing, or exam search returns no results because symptom panel provides display names not FONASA codes.
5. BUG 5 (RUT prompt): `context.patientRut` is empty/null despite user being in DB. `mainProfile.rut` doesn't exist on User model. Need to populate from patient records by phone.

observations.title: Guarded Shuri purchase confirmations from package routing
observations.content:
**What**: Added a confirmation/generation guard in Shuri routing so active purchase stages are not hijacked by package recommendation intent, protected `userChangedIntent` from resetting purchase flow on confirmation messages, and fixed campaign flow read path to include `memory.purchase.campaignFlow`.
**Why**: Users replying with confirmations like "si, generala" during order purchase were being routed to package suggestions instead of continuing order generation.
**Where**: apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/shuri-agent.ts; apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/campaign-intent-detector.ts
**Learned**: `isPackageRecommendation` executes before `awaiting_exam_list` handling, so explicit stage-aware guards are required to preserve purchase continuity when intent classification is noisy.

observations.title: Session summary: Examya
observations.content:
## Goal
Investigate three Shuri WhatsApp conversation bugs using source-level analysis.

## Instructions
Return structured technical findings in English with root cause, why it happens, and suggested code fixes per problem.

## Discoveries
- Campaign flow persistence is broken by a path mismatch: detector reads `context.memory.campaignFlow`, while Shuri stores campaign state under `response.purchase.campaignFlow`.
- Intent classification is contextless and cache-keyed only by normalized message text, so short confirmations like "si, generala" can be misclassified and reused globally.
- Routing order in `ShuriAgent` allows package recommendation handling before purchase-stage handling, so confirmations can be diverted before order generation logic runs.
- `STAGE_PROMPTS` uses `awaiting_examType` while runtime uses `awaiting_exam_type` in `interpret`, forcing fallback behavior for that stage.

## Accomplished
- ✅ Read all requested files and traced flow persistence, intent detection, and routing execution order.
- ✅ Located exact code locations that explain the 3 reported issues.
- ✅ Prepared concrete fix strategy per issue (state path alignment, context-aware intent handling/cache, and routing precedence guards).

## Next Steps
- Implement the proposed fixes and add regression tests for campaign flow continuity and "si, generala" confirmation path.

## Relevant Files
- apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/handlers/campaign-handler.ts — campaign flow entry/continuation behavior
- apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/campaign-intent-detector.ts — button detection and campaign flow state lookup
- apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/intent-router.ts — LLM/regex intent classification and cache keying
- apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/intent-router.types.ts — intent taxonomy
- apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/conversational-engine.ts — stage prompts and interpretation fallback
- apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/shuri-agent.ts — orchestration, stage machine, and routing precedence
- apps/agents/src/routes/shuri.ts — context read/write integration around ShuriAgent
- apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/shuri-memory.ts — Redis/DB context persistence model

observations.title: Diagnosed Shuri flow and intent misrouting
observations.content:
**What**: Identified root causes in Shuri WhatsApp flow: campaign state path mismatch, contextless/cached intent classification for confirmation messages, and routing precedence that sends confirmations to package recommendations.
**Why**: User reported that flow context was lost (`existingFlow: null`), and explicit confirmation (`si, generala`) was misrouted instead of generating an order.
**Where**: apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/campaign-intent-detector.ts, apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/shuri-agent.ts, apps/agents/src/routes/shuri.ts, apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/shuri-memory.ts, apps/agents/src/agents/shuri/conversational-engine.ts.
**Learned**: `campaignFlow` is persisted under `purchase` but read from `memory.campaignFlow`; IntentRouter cache key uses only raw message text, so short confirmations can be globally misclassified and reused across contexts.
